_note: Fixed calibration anchors for the adversarial Turing-judge. Each run, mix these two blind with the batch under test. The judge MUST rank human_messy far above ai_slop (expected gap >= 40 pts). If it does not, its scoring is unreliable THIS run -> discard the absolute score and trust only the relative A/B verdict. Both anchors are authored in-house (no external/copyrighted text).

calibration_rule.on_fail: mark run absolute-score UNTRUSTED; use relative A/B verdict only

human_messy.why: maximally human forum: typos, inconsistent caps, a tangent, an UNANSWERED question, mild hostility, a bare +1, a broken-off sentence, someone plainly wrong, an argument nobody concedes

posts.title: [ASK] does the 6am shuttle actually run sundays or
posts.author: u/parked_again
posts.body: ok this is gonna sound dumb but does the early one actually run on sundays. the posted schedule says yes but i stood there 25 min last week and nothing showed. am i misreading it or is the sign just wrong. genuinely asking before i do that to myself again

posts.title: [SHOW] fence is finally done
posts.author: u/three_weekends
posts.body: took me way longer than it should have but the back fence is up. one post is a little crooked and i have decided not to see it. theres leftover pickets by the shed if anyone wants them, just take em

posts.title: [RANT] whoever keeps leaving the side gate open
posts.author: u/notmydog_actually
posts.body: not naming names. the dog got out AGAIN and somehow its my dog getting blamed when its not even. shut the gate. pull it TO. thats the whole post

comments.author: u/rides_it
comments.body: the sunday run is a different time, its the 6:40 not the 6:00, they never fixed the sign. you werent misreading it the sign is just wrong

comments.author: u/gave_up_too
comments.body: +1 waited like half an hour once and bailed

comments.author: u/every_sunday

comments.author: u/needs_pickets
comments.body: are the pickets still there. can swing by after work if so

comments.author: u/seen_things
comments.body: its probably the henderson kid, i see him mess with it

comments.author: u/hinge_guy
comments.body: its not a person its the wind, the latch doesnt catch on its own. put a spring hinge on it and youll never think about it again

comments.author: u/notmydog_actually
comments.body: its not the wind ive literally watched it swing shut and stay. someone is not pulling it to. a spring hinge is not the point

votes.note: downvotes on the accusation comment + the wrong every_sunday reply

ai_slop.why: maximally AI: one polished voice across all handles, every post a tidy vignette ending on an aphorism, identical cadence, zero typos, no unresolved conflict, everyone uniformly gracious

posts.title: [STORY] The Lantern by the Door
posts.author: u/quiet_keeper
posts.body: Tonight I lit the lantern by the door, as I do each evening. The small flame pushed gently back against the dark, and I found myself thinking of all who had lit it before me, and all who would light it after. We are, each of us, keepers of a small and borrowed light. And perhaps that is enough.

posts.title: [SHOW] On Mending the Gate
posts.author: u/steady_hands
posts.body: Today I mended the garden gate. The hinge had worn thin, as all things wear thin in their time. I filed it true and hung it straight again. There is a quiet dignity in tending faithfully to what we are given. The gate holds now; and so, I think, do we.

posts.title: [REFLECTION] What the River Knows
posts.author: u/still_water
posts.body: I sat beside the river this morning and simply watched it pass. It asks for nothing and it keeps nothing. Perhaps wisdom is only this in the end: to move as the river moves, and to let each moment go as it comes. The water remembers nothing, and so the water is never troubled.

comments.author: u/gentle_reader
comments.body: Beautifully put. The lantern is such a fitting image for the way we quietly carry one another through the dark.

comments.author: u/kind_soul
comments.body: This resonates with me deeply. There is indeed a rare dignity in small and faithfully tended things.

comments.author: u/soft_light
comments.body: What a lovely and gentle reflection. The river as a patient teacher is an image I will carry with me all through today.

comments.author: u/warm_regards
comments.body: Thank you for sharing something so tender. It is a genuine gift to be reminded of the quiet and enduring things.
